raw_response:
Short sharp pain of tooth transmitted to brain by A-delta fibers.
\boxed{A} To explain, the dental pain involving sharp sensations like drilling or biting activates A-delta fibers, characterized by being myelinated, lightly so, allowing for quick propagation of the pain signal to the brain.
\boxed{A}
Your clinical reasoning aligns closely with the given evidence. Sharp, sudden pains experienced around teeth are indeed mediated primarily by A-delta fibers because they are responsible for carrying fast pain signals due to their higher conduction speed relative to unmyelinated C fibers. Therefore, option \( \boxed{A} \) correctly identifies A-delta fibers as the ones involved in transmitting short, sharp tooth pain to the brain. Well done!
